rulings.Case Title: JOHN W MILLER et al VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 20, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant J-M Manufacturing Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence sufficient to create a triable issue whether decedent John Miller developed an asbestos-related illness. Defendant's objections to Dr. Gourley's declaration are sustained as to paragraphs 3, 7-12 (Garibay v. Hemmat (2008) 161 Cal.App.4th 735) and overruled as to paragraphs 2, 4-6. Paragraphs 13-16 are moot given the ruling on the motion for summary judgment. There is no argument that defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD MCLAIN et al VS. CRANE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 20, 2017 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Sid E. Parker Boiler Mfg. Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence to create a triable issue whether decedent Richard McLain was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. The Court did not consider defendant's reply papers for failure to lodge a courtesy copy with the Court. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
